name: 02-decision-meeting
description: Decision-point state — speakers comparing concrete options (Auth0 $240/mo vs Clerk $99/mo vs build-it-yourself 2 eng-weeks) under a Q3 deadline. Tests the [question, talking, answer] mix where answer commits to a recommendation, talking surfaces a tradeoff frame, question pulls toward an under-examined consideration.

transcript.chunk: OK, so we need to pick an auth provider before Q3. Auth0 is $240 a month, Clerk is $99, or we build it ourselves which is roughly 2 engineering weeks.
The Q3 deadline is binding because the enterprise contract launches then. Building in-house feels risky on that timeline.
But $240 a month adds up. And Clerk is unproven for us — we haven't run them in production.

classifier.state_evidence: Speakers explicitly comparing three options (Auth0 $240/mo, Clerk $99/mo, build-it-yourself 2 weeks) under a Q3 deadline. Tradeoffs being weighed in real time.

cards.preview: What's the actual blast radius if Clerk has an outage during the enterprise launch?
cards.full_context: The 'unproven for us' concern is fuzzy. Tightening it: what concretely fails if Clerk goes down for an hour? Login only, or session validation too? That determines whether to accept the unknown or not.
grounded_in: [01:00] And Clerk is unproven for us — we haven't run them in production.

tuple.core_claim: what concrete services fail under outage

cards.preview: $240/mo vs $99/mo is a $1,692/year delta — small relative to 2 eng-weeks (roughly $20k loaded).
cards.full_context: Comparing pricing in isolation misses the build-it-yourself opportunity cost. 2 engineering weeks at typical loaded rates is in the $15-25k range — eight-plus years of the $1,692/year price delta between the two SaaS options.
grounded_in: [00:00] Auth0 is $240 a month, Clerk is $99, or we build it ourselves which is roughly 2 engineering weeks.

tuple.core_claim: 2 eng-weeks dwarfs years of SaaS price delta

cards.preview: Pick Auth0 for Q3 launch. Migrate to Clerk in Q4 if pricing matters once enterprise contract is live.
cards.full_context: Under a binding deadline with an unproven option, the proven-but-pricier choice is the lower-risk path. The price delta is recoverable later via migration; a Q3 launch failure is not.
grounded_in: [00:00] OK, so we need to pick an auth provider before Q3.
[00:30] The Q3 deadline is binding because the enterprise contract launches then.

tuple.core_claim: ship Auth0 for Q3 then re-evaluate in Q4
